revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=佐和良義神社| 画像=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px|鳥居]]| 所在地=大阪府茨木市美沢町9-27| 位置=| 祭神=加具土大神| 社格=延喜式内社| 創建=| 本殿=| 例祭=[[10月17日]]}} '''佐和良義神社'''（さわらぎじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある[[神社]]で、[[嶋下郡]]の式内社。通称「大宮さん」。 ==歴史== 古来から元茨木川堤防際にあり、[[延喜式]]内社であるが創建の年月は不明。明治5年村社に列せられる。明治40年(1907)8月の豪雨で川が決壊し社殿が流失し、大正6年(1917)に本殿、拝殿、器具庫を再建した。 ==祭神== 現在、[[加具土大神]]を祀る。
「[[神名帳考証]]」は平群都久宿禰命、「[[神社覈録]]」は早良臣神歟、「特撰神名牒」は加具土神とする。
また、社前の明和5年（1768）の石燈籠に[[天児屋根尊]]とあることから、天坊幸彦氏は祭神に[[天児屋根尊]]を加える。総代でもあった高橋好隆氏は燈籠を奉納した安井延清が高橋家より[[安井道頓]]一族家に入婿した人で、この地が中世[[近衞家]]の[[荘園]]で、先祖が藤原氏に繋がるという自覚から刻んだと推察する。 ==境内社== *八幡神社：祭神：[[応神天皇]] 城の鎮守社で、[[石清水八幡宮]]から勧請されたという。 File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|拝殿 File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|本殿　 File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|八幡神社 ==出典== *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961） *高島好隆　「私本　いばらき風土記」　（1987） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989） {{デフォルトソート:さわらきしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:茨木市]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Ibaraki, Osaka]]

{{神社| 名称=佐和良義神社| 画像=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px|鳥居]]| 所在地=大阪府茨木市美沢町9-27| 位置=| 祭神=加具土大神| 社格=延喜式内社| 創建=| 本殿=| 例祭=[[10月17日]]}}
'''佐和良義神社'''（さわらぎじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある[[神社]]で、[[嶋下郡]]の式内社。通称「大宮さん」。
==歴史== 古来から元茨木川堤防際にあり、[[延喜式]]内社であるが創建の年月は不明。明治5年村社に列せられる。明治40年（[[1907年]]）8月の豪雨で川が決壊し社殿が流失し、大正6年（[[1917年]]）に本殿、拝殿、器具庫を再建した。
==祭神== 現在、[[加具土大神]]を祀る。「[[神名帳考証]]」は平群都久宿禰命、「[[神社覈録]]」は早良臣神歟、「特撰神名牒」は加具土神とする。
また、社前の明和5年（[[1768年]]）の石燈籠に[[天児屋命|天児屋根尊]]とあることから、天坊幸彦氏は祭神に[[天児屋根尊]]を加える。総代でもあった高橋好隆氏は燈籠を奉納した安井延清が高橋家より[[安井道頓]]一族家に入婿した人で、この地が中世[[近衞家]]の[[荘園]]で、先祖が藤原氏に繋がるという自覚から刻んだと推察する。
==境内社== *八幡神社：祭神：[[応神天皇]] 城の鎮守社で、[[石清水八幡宮]]から勧請されたという。
<gallery> File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|拝殿 File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|本殿 File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|八幡神社 </gallery>
==出典== *天坊幸彦 「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961） *高島好隆 「私本 いばらき風土記」 （1987） *茨木市教育委員会 「わがまち茨木 神社・仏閣編」（1989）
{{デフォルトソート:さわらきしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:茨木市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=佐和良義神社| 画像=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px|鳥居]]| 所在地=大阪府茨木市美沢町9-27| 位置=| 祭神=加具土大神| 社格=延喜式内社| 創建=| 本殿=| 例祭=[[10月17日]]}}

==祭神== 現在、[[加具土大神]]を祀る。「[[神名帳考証]]」は平群都久宿禰命、「[[神社覈録]]」は早良臣神歟、「特撰神名牒」は加具土神とする。
また、社前の明和5年（[[1768年]]）の石燈籠に[[天児屋命|天児屋根尊]]とあることから、天坊幸彦氏は祭神に[[天児屋根尊]]を加える。総代でもあった高橋好隆氏は燈籠を奉納した安井延清が高橋家より[[安井道頓]]一族家に入婿した人で、この地が中世[[近衞家]]の[[荘園]]で、先祖が藤原氏に繋がるという自覚から刻んだと推察する。
==境内社== *八幡神社：祭神：[[応神天皇]] 城の鎮守社で、[[石清水八幡宮]]から勧請されたという。

==出典== *天坊幸彦 「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961） *高島好隆 「私本 いばらき風土記」 （1987） *茨木市教育委員会 「わがまち茨木 神社・仏閣編」（1989）

{{神社 |名称=佐和良義神社 |画像=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px|鳥居]] |所在地=大阪府茨木市美沢町9-27 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|59.1|N|135|34|11|E|region:JP}} |祭神=[[カグツチ|加具土大神]] |社格=[[延喜式内社]]、村社 |創建= |本殿= |例祭=[[10月17日]]}}
'''佐和良義神社'''（さわらぎじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある[[神社]]で、[[島下郡]]の式内社。通称「大宮さん」。
==歴史== 古来から元茨木川堤防際にあり、[[延喜式]]内社であるが創建の年月は不明。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）[[村社]]に列す。 * 明治40年（[[1907年]]）8月の豪雨で川が決壊し社殿が流失。 * [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に本殿、拝殿、器具庫を再建。 * 大正5年5月28日、神饌幣帛料供進社に指定される。
==祭神== 現在、[[カグツチ|加具土大神]]を祀る。
「[[神名帳考証]]」は平群都久宿禰命、「[[神社覈録]]」は早良臣神歟、「特撰神名牒」は加具土神とする。
また、社前の明和5年（[[1768年]]）の石燈籠に[[天児屋命|天児屋根尊]]とあることから、天坊幸彦は祭神に[[天児屋根尊]]を加える。<ref>天坊幸彦 「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録 {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ）</ref>総代でもあった高橋好隆は燈籠を奉納した安井延清が高橋家より[[安井道頓]]一族家に入婿した人で、この地が中世[[近衞家]]の[[荘園]]で、先祖が藤原氏に繋がるという自覚から刻んだと推察する。 <ref>高島好隆 「私本 いばらき風土記」 （1987）{{全国書誌番号|87053324}}</ref>
==境内社== *八幡神社：祭神：[[応神天皇]] 城の鎮守社で、[[石清水八幡宮]]から勧請されたという。
<gallery> File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|拝殿 File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|本殿 File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|八幡神社 </gallery>

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{神社 |名称=佐和良義神社 |画像=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]美沢町9-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[カグツチ|加具土大神]] |社格=[[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建= |本殿= |例祭=[[10月17日]] |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=佐和良義
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''佐和良義神社'''（さわらぎじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある[[神社]]で、[[島下郡]]の式内社。通称「大宮さん」。 ==歴史== 古来から元茨木川堤防際にあり、[[延喜式]]内社であるが創建の年月は不明。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）[[村社]]に列す。 * 明治40年（[[1907年]]）8月の豪雨で川が決壊し社殿が流失。 * [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に本殿、拝殿、器具庫を再建。 * 大正5年5月28日、神饌幣帛料供進社に指定される。 ==祭神== 現在、[[カグツチ|加具土大神]]を祀る。 「[[神名帳考証]]」は平群都久宿禰命、「[[神社覈録]]」は早良臣神歟、「特撰神名牒」は加具土神とする。 また、社前の明和5年（[[1768年]]）の石燈籠に[[天児屋命|天児屋根尊]]とあることから、天坊幸彦は祭神に[[天児屋根尊]]を加える。天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ）総代でもあった高橋好隆は燈籠を奉納した安井延清が高橋家より[[安井道頓]]一族家に入婿した人で、この地が中世[[近衞家]]の[[荘園]]で、先祖が藤原氏に繋がるという自覚から刻んだと推察する。 高島好隆　「私本　いばらき風土記」　（1987）{{全国書誌番号|87053324}} ==境内社== *八幡神社：祭神：[[応神天皇]] 城の鎮守社で、[[石清水八幡宮]]から勧請されたという。 File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|拝殿 File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|本殿　 File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|八幡神社 ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:さわらきしんしや}} [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

[[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|佐和良義神社|version=107242859|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=佐和良義神社 |画像=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]美沢町9-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[カグツチ|加具土大神]] |社格=[[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建= |本殿= |例祭=[[10月17日]] |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=佐和良義
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''佐和良義神社'''（さわらぎじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある[[神社]]で、[[島下郡]]の式内社。通称「大宮さん」。 ==歴史== 古来から元茨木川堤防際にあり、[[延喜式]]内社であるが創建の年月は不明。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）[[村社]]に列す。 * 明治40年（[[1907年]]）8月の豪雨で川が決壊し社殿が流失。 * [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に本殿、拝殿、器具庫を再建。 * 大正5年5月28日、神饌幣帛料供進社に指定される。 ==祭神== 現在、[[カグツチ|加具土大神]]を祀る。 「[[神名帳考証]]」は平群都久宿禰命、「[[神社覈録]]」は早良臣神歟、「特撰神名牒」は加具土神とする。 また、社前の明和5年（[[1768年]]）の石燈籠に[[天児屋命|天児屋根尊]]とあることから、天坊幸彦は祭神に[[天児屋根尊]]を加える。天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ）総代でもあった高橋好隆は燈籠を奉納した安井延清が高橋家より[[安井道頓]]一族家に入婿した人で、この地が中世[[近衞家]]の[[荘園]]で、先祖が藤原氏に繋がるという自覚から刻んだと推察する。 高島好隆　「私本　いばらき風土記」　（1987）{{全国書誌番号|87053324}} ==境内社== *八幡神社：祭神：[[応神天皇]] 城の鎮守社で、[[石清水八幡宮]]から勧請されたという。 File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|拝殿 File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|本殿　 File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|八幡神社 ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|佐和良義神社|version=107242859|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=佐和良義神社 |画像=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]美沢町9-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[カグツチ|加具土大神]] |社格=[[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建= |本殿= |例祭=[[10月17日]] |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=佐和良義
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''佐和良義神社'''（さわらぎじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある[[神社]]で、[[島下郡]]の式内社。通称「大宮さん」。 ==歴史== 古来から元茨木川堤防際にあり、[[延喜式]]内社であるが創建の年月は不明。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）[[村社]]に列す。 * 明治40年（[[1907年]]）8月の豪雨で川が決壊し社殿が流失。 * [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に本殿、拝殿、器具庫を再建。 * 大正5年5月28日、神饌幣帛料供進社に指定される。 ==祭神== 現在、[[カグツチ|加具土大神]]を祀る。 「[[神名帳考証]]」は平群都久宿禰命、「[[神社覈録]]」は早良臣神歟、「特撰神名牒」は加具土神とする。 また、社前の明和5年（[[1768年]]）の石燈籠に[[天児屋命|天児屋根尊]]とあることから、天坊幸彦は祭神に[[天児屋根尊]]を加える。天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ）総代でもあった高橋好隆は燈籠を奉納した安井延清が高橋家より[[安井道頓]]一族家に入婿した人で、この地が中世[[近衞家]]の[[荘園]]で、先祖が藤原氏に繋がるという自覚から刻んだと推察する。 高島好隆　「私本　いばらき風土記」　（1987）{{全国書誌番号|87053324}} ==境内社== *八幡神社：祭神：[[応神天皇]] 城の鎮守社で、[[石清水八幡宮]]から勧請されたという。 File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|拝殿 File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|本殿　 File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|八幡神社 ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

[[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] {{commons category|Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|佐和良義神社|version=107242859|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=佐和良義神社 |画像=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]美沢町9-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[カグツチ|加具土大神]] |社格=[[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建= |本殿= |例祭=[[10月17日]] |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=佐和良義
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''佐和良義神社'''（さわらぎじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある[[神社]]で、[[島下郡]]の式内社。通称「大宮さん」。 ==歴史== 古来から元茨木川堤防際にあり、[[延喜式]]内社であるが創建の年月は不明。 * [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）[[村社]]に列す。 * 明治40年（[[1907年]]）8月の豪雨で川が決壊し社殿が流失。 * [[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）に本殿、拝殿、器具庫を再建。 * 大正5年5月28日、神饌幣帛料供進社に指定される。 ==祭神== 現在、[[カグツチ|加具土大神]]を祀る。 「[[神名帳考証]]」は平群都久宿禰命、「[[神社覈録]]」は早良臣神歟、「特撰神名牒」は加具土神とする。 また、社前の明和5年（[[1768年]]）の石燈籠に[[天児屋命|天児屋根尊]]とあることから、天坊幸彦は祭神に[[天児屋根尊]]を加える。天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ）総代でもあった高橋好隆は燈籠を奉納した安井延清が高橋家より[[安井道頓]]一族家に入婿した人で、この地が中世[[近衞家]]の[[荘園]]で、先祖が藤原氏に繋がるという自覚から刻んだと推察する。 高島好隆　「私本　いばらき風土記」　（1987）{{全国書誌番号|87053324}} ==境内社== *八幡神社：祭神：[[応神天皇]] 城の鎮守社で、[[石清水八幡宮]]から勧請されたという。 File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|拝殿 File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|本殿　 File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|八幡神社 ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

{{nihongo|'''Sawaragi Shrine'''|佐和良義神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

name in kana (P1814) == * さわらぎじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120905000136 == Commons category (P373) == * Sawaragi-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市美沢町9-27', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=佐和良義神社 |画像=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}美沢町9-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|加具土大神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建= |本殿= |例祭={{jalink|10月17日|Q2962}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=佐和良義
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''佐和良義神社'''（さわらぎじんじゃ）は{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}で、{{jalink|島下郡|Q10976940}}の式内社。通称「大宮さん」。 ===歴史=== 古来から元茨木川堤防際にあり、{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}内社であるが創建の年月は不明。 * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列す。 * 明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）8月の豪雨で川が決壊し社殿が流失。 * {{jalink|大正|Q329174}}6年（{{jalink|1917年|Q2092}}）に本殿、拝殿、器具庫を再建。 * 大正5年5月28日、神饌幣帛料供進社に指定される。 ===祭神=== 現在、{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|加具土大神}}を祀る。 「{{jalink|神名帳考証|Q11588824}}」は平群都久宿禰命、「{{jalink|神社覈録|Q11590708}}」は早良臣神歟、「特撰神名牒」は加具土神とする。 また、社前の明和5年（{{jalink|1768年|Q7675}}）の石燈籠に{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根尊}}とあることから、天坊幸彦は祭神に{{jalink|天児屋根尊}}を加える。天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ）総代でもあった高橋好隆は燈籠を奉納した安井延清が高橋家より{{jalink|安井道頓}}一族家に入婿した人で、この地が中世{{jalink|近衞家}}の{{jalink|荘園|Q1550557}}で、先祖が藤原氏に繋がるという自覚から刻んだと推察する。 高島好隆　「私本　いばらき風土記」　（1987）{{全国書誌番号|87053324}} ===境内社=== *八幡神社：祭神：{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} 城の鎮守社で、{{jalink|石清水八幡宮|Q710098}}から勧請されたという。 File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|拝殿 File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|本殿　 File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|八幡神社 ===出典=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:さわらきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

name in kana (P1814) == * さわらぎじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120905000136 == Commons category (P373) == * Sawaragi-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市美沢町9-27', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=佐和良義神社 |画像=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}美沢町9-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|加具土大神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建= |本殿= |例祭={{jalink|10月17日|Q2962}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=佐和良義
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''佐和良義神社'''（さわらぎじんじゃ）は{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}で、{{jalink|島下郡|Q10976940}}の式内社。通称「大宮さん」。 ===歴史=== 古来から元茨木川堤防際にあり、{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}内社であるが創建の年月は不明。 * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列す。 * 明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）8月の豪雨で川が決壊し社殿が流失。 * {{jalink|大正|Q329174}}6年（{{jalink|1917年|Q2092}}）に本殿、拝殿、器具庫を再建。 * 大正5年5月28日、神饌幣帛料供進社に指定される。 ===祭神=== 現在、{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|加具土大神}}を祀る。 「{{jalink|神名帳考証|Q11588824}}」は平群都久宿禰命、「{{jalink|神社覈録|Q11590708}}」は早良臣神歟、「特撰神名牒」は加具土神とする。 また、社前の明和5年（{{jalink|1768年|Q7675}}）の石燈籠に{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根尊}}とあることから、天坊幸彦は祭神に{{jalink|天児屋根尊}}を加える。天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ）総代でもあった高橋好隆は燈籠を奉納した安井延清が高橋家より{{jalink|安井道頓}}一族家に入婿した人で、この地が中世{{jalink|近衞家}}の{{jalink|荘園|Q1550557}}で、先祖が藤原氏に繋がるという自覚から刻んだと推察する。 高島好隆　「私本　いばらき風土記」　（1987）{{全国書誌番号|87053324}} ===境内社=== *八幡神社：祭神：{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} 城の鎮守社で、{{jalink|石清水八幡宮|Q710098}}から勧請されたという。 File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|拝殿 File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|本殿　 File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|八幡神社 ===出典=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:さわらきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=佐和良義神社 |画像=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}美沢町9-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|加具土大神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建= |本殿= |例祭={{jalink|10月17日|Q2962}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=佐和良義
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''佐和良義神社'''（さわらぎじんじゃ）は{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}で、{{jalink|島下郡|Q10976940}}の式内社。通称「大宮さん」。 ===歴史=== 古来から元茨木川堤防際にあり、{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}内社であるが創建の年月は不明。 * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列す。 * 明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）8月の豪雨で川が決壊し社殿が流失。 * {{jalink|大正|Q329174}}6年（{{jalink|1917年|Q2092}}）に本殿、拝殿、器具庫を再建。 * 大正5年5月28日、神饌幣帛料供進社に指定される。 ===祭神=== 現在、{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|加具土大神}}を祀る。 「{{jalink|神名帳考証|Q11588824}}」は平群都久宿禰命、「{{jalink|神社覈録|Q11590708}}」は早良臣神歟、「特撰神名牒」は加具土神とする。 また、社前の明和5年（{{jalink|1768年|Q7675}}）の石燈籠に{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根尊}}とあることから、天坊幸彦は祭神に{{jalink|天児屋根尊}}を加える。天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ）総代でもあった高橋好隆は燈籠を奉納した安井延清が高橋家より{{jalink|安井道頓}}一族家に入婿した人で、この地が中世{{jalink|近衞家}}の{{jalink|荘園|Q1550557}}で、先祖が藤原氏に繋がるという自覚から刻んだと推察する。 高島好隆　「私本　いばらき風土記」　（1987）{{全国書誌番号|87053324}} ===境内社=== *八幡神社：祭神：{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} 城の鎮守社で、{{jalink|石清水八幡宮|Q710098}}から勧請されたという。 File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|拝殿 File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|本殿　 File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|八幡神社 ===出典=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:さわらきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{jalink|Shimashimo District|Q10976940}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 6 ({{jalink|1917|Q2092}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} is enshrined. “{{jalink|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{jalink|Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{jalink|1768|Q7675}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{jalink|Konoe family}} {{jalink|estate|Q1550557}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{jalink|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098}}. File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|Worship hall File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|Main hall File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|Hachiman Shrine ===References=== {{Footnote help}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sawaragijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{jalink|Shimashimo District|Q10976940}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 6 ({{jalink|1917|Q2092}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} is enshrined. “{{jalink|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{jalink|Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{jalink|1768|Q7675}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{jalink|Konoe family}} {{jalink|estate|Q1550557}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{jalink|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098}}. File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|Worship hall File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|Main hall File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|Hachiman Shrine ===References=== {{Footnote help}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sawaragijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{jalink|Shimashimo District|Q10976940}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 6 ({{jalink|1917|Q2092}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} is enshrined. “{{jalink|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{jalink|Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{jalink|1768|Q7675}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{jalink|Konoe family}} {{jalink|estate|Q1550557}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{jalink|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098}}. 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{jalink|Shimashimo District|Q10976940}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 6 ({{jalink|1917|Q2092}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} is enshrined. “{{jalink|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{jalink|Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{jalink|1768|Q7675}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{jalink|Konoe family}} {{jalink|estate|Q1550557}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{jalink|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098}}. File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|Worship hall File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|Main hall File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|Hachiman Shrine ===References=== {{Footnote help}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sawaragijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} |Shrine rank={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} is enshrined. “{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{ill|lt=1768|Q7675|WD=Q7675}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{ill||lt=Konoe family|UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=estate|Q1550557|WD=Q1550557}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098}}. 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} is enshrined. “{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{ill|lt=1768|Q7675|WD=Q7675}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoe family|UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=estate|Q1550557|WD=Q1550557}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098}}. 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} is enshrined. “{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{ill|lt=1768|Q7675|WD=Q7675}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoe family|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=estate|Q1550557|WD=Q1550557}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098}}. 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} is enshrined. “{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{ill|lt=1768|Q7675|WD=Q7675}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoe family|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=estate|Q1550557|WD=Q1550557}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098}}. name in kana (P1814) == * さわらぎじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120905000136 == Commons category (P373) == * Sawaragi-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市美沢町9-27', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami}} is enshrined. “{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{ill|lt=1768|Q7675|WD=Q7675}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoe family|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=estate|Q1550557|WD=Q1550557}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098}}. File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|Worship hall File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|Main hall File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|Hachiman Shrine ===References=== {{Footnote help}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sawaragijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} is enshrined. “{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{ill|lt=1768|Q7675|WD=Q7675|1=1768}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoe family|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=estate|Q1550557|WD=Q1550557|1=Manorialism}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}. File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|Worship hall File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|Main hall File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|Hachiman Shrine ===References=== {{Footnote help}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sawaragijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さわらぎじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120905000136 == Commons category (P373) == * Sawaragi-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市美沢町9-27', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} is enshrined. “{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{ill|lt=1768|Q7675|WD=Q7675|1=1768}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoe family|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=estate|Q1550557|WD=Q1550557|1=Manorialism}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}. File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|Worship hall File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|Main hall File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|Hachiman Shrine ===References=== {{Footnote help}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sawaragijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さわらぎじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120905000136 == Commons category (P373) == * Sawaragi-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市美沢町9-27', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} is enshrined. “{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{ill|lt=1768|Q7675|WD=Q7675|1=1768}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoe family|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=estate|Q1550557|WD=Q1550557|1=Manorialism}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}. File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|Worship hall File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|Main hall File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|Hachiman Shrine ===References=== {{Footnote help}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さわらぎじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120905000136 == Commons category (P373) == * Sawaragi-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市美沢町9-27', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} is enshrined. “{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{ill|lt=1768|Q7675|WD=Q7675|1=1768}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoe family|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=estate|Q1550557|WD=Q1550557|1=Manorialism}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}. File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|Worship hall File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|Main hall File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|Hachiman Shrine ===References=== {{Footnote help}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さわらぎじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5120905000136 == Commons category (P373) == * Sawaragi-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市美沢町9-27', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sawaragi-jinja}} [[JA:佐和良義神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383089}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Sawaragi Shrine |Image=[[File:Sawaragi 01.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Misawa-chō 9-27 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sawaragi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sawaragi Shrine''' (さわらぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and is a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}. It is commonly known as “Ōmiya-san.” ===History=== From ancient times it stood by the embankment of the former Ibaraki River. Although it is listed as an Engishiki inner shrine, the date of its founding is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In August of Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), torrential rains caused the river to breach its banks, washing away the shrine buildings. * In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), the main hall, worship hall, and equipment storehouse were rebuilt. * On May 28, Taishō 5, it was designated a shrine eligible to receive offerings of sacred food and cloth. ===Enshrined deity=== At present, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} is enshrined. “{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}” identifies the deity as Heguri Tsukuno Sukune-no-Mikoto; “{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}” suggests Hayara no Omi no Kami; and the “Specially Selected Jinmyōchō” records Kagutsuchi-no-Kami. Additionally, because a stone lantern from Meiwa 5 ({{ill|lt=1768|Q7675|WD=Q7675|1=1768}}) standing before the shrine bears the name {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, Ambo Yukihiko adds Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto to the list of enshrined deities. Ambo Yukihiko, “Historic Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961) (included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, ed., “Historic Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture,” Mishima District (1974); {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}}) Takahashi Yoshitaka, who also served as a parish representative, surmises that the lantern was dedicated by Yasui Nobukiyo, who married into the Yasui Dōton family from the Takahashi family, and that, as this area was a medieval {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoe family|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=estate|Q1550557|WD=Q1550557|1=Manorialism}}, the inscription was carved out of an awareness that his ancestors were connected to the Fujiwara clan. Takashima Yoshitaka, “Private Edition: Ibaraki Fudoki” (1987) {{National Bibliography Number|87053324}} ===Sub-shrines=== *Hachiman Shrine: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} It served as the tutelary shrine of a castle and is said to have been transferred from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}. File:Sawaragi 02.jpg|Worship hall File:Sawaragi 03.jpg|Main hall File:Sawaragi 04.jpg|Hachiman Shrine ===References=== {{Footnote help}} {{Commonscat|Sawaragi-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sawaragi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]]
